description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: Democratic politician from California
description_wiki: Horace Jeremiah "Jerry" Voorhis (April 6, 1901 – September 11, 1984) was an American politician and educator who served five terms in the United States House of Representatives representing California from 1937 to 1947.

description: bicameral legislature of the United States
description_wiki: The United States Congress is the legislative branch of the federal government of the United States.

description: public university system for North Carolina, USA
description_wiki: The University of North Carolina is the public university system for the state of North Carolina.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: Lower house of Texas’ legislature
description_wiki: The Texas House of Representatives is the lower house of the bicameral Texas Legislature.

description: phenomenon in the United States of making accusations of subversion or treason without proper regard for evidence, named after Senator Joseph McCarthy
description_wiki: McCarthyism is a political practice defined by the political repression and persecution of left-wing individuals and a campaign spreading fear of communist and Soviet influence on American institutions and of Soviet espionage in the United States during the late 1940s through the 1950s, heavily associated with the Second Red Scare, also known as the McCarthy era.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.

description: president of the United States from 1963 to 1969 (1908–1973)
description_wiki: Lyndon Baines Johnson (August 27, 1908 – January 22, 1973), also known as LBJ, was the 36th president of the United States from 1963 until 1969.

description: American advocacy group
description_wiki: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is an American nonprofit civil rights organization founded in 1920.

description: Quaker organization
description_wiki: The American Friends Service Committee (AFSC) is a Quaker-founded organization working for peace and social justice in the United States and around the world.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: American politician (1895-1954)
description_wiki: Fontaine Maury Maverick Sr.

description: American statesman and governor (1873–1944)
description_wiki: Alfred Emanuel  Smith (December 30, 1873 – October 4, 1944) was an American politician who served as the 42nd governor of New York from 1919 to 1920 and again from 1923 to 1928.

description: American white supremacist hate group
description_wiki: The Ku Klux Klan (KKK; ), sometimes referred to as the Klan, is an American Protestant-led white supremacist and far-right hate group.

description: city in Bexar, Comal, and Medina counties in Texas, United States, that is the seat of Bexar County
description_wiki: San Antonio ( SAN an-TOH-nee-oh; Spanish for "Saint Anthony") is a city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: seat of Harris County, and largest city in State of Texas, United States
description_wiki: Houston (  HEW-stən) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Texas and the Southern United States.

description: American politician and entrepreneur (1874–1956)
description_wiki: Jesse Holman Jones (April 5, 1874 – June 1, 1956) was an American Democratic politician and entrepreneur from Houston, Texas.

description: dictator of Germany from 1933 to 1945, main instigator of World War II (1889–1945)
description_wiki: Adolf Hitler (20 April 1889 – 30 April 1945) was an Austrian-born  German politician who was the dictator of Germany during the Nazi era from 1933 until his suicide in 1945.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: U.S. Supreme Court justice (1886-1971)
description_wiki: Hugo Lafayette Black (February 27, 1886 – September 25, 1971) was an American lawyer, politician, and jurist who served as a U.S. Senator from Alabama from 1927 to 1937 and as an associate justice of the U.S. Supreme Court from 1937 to 1971.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

aliases: The University of Texas at Austin
description: public university in Austin, Texas, USA
description_wiki: The University of Texas at Austin (UT Austin, UT, or Texas) is a public research university in Austin, Texas, United States.

description: Loyola Marymount University's law school in Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: Loyola Law School is the law school of Loyola Marymount University, a private Jesuit university in Los Angeles, California.

entity: St. Mary's University School of Law
surface_forms: St. Mary's Law School in San Antonio
description: Private Catholic law school in San Antonio, Texas, USA

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: president of the United States from 1969 to 1974 (1913–1994)
description_wiki: Richard Milhous Nixon (January 9, 1913 – April 22, 1994) was the 37th president of the United States, serving from 1969 until his resignation in 1974.

description: American senator from Texas and chairman of the Tower Commission (1925-1991)
description_wiki: John Goodwin Tower (September 29, 1925 – April 5, 1991) was an American politician and military veteran who represented Texas in the United States Senate from 1961 to 1985.

description: county in Texas, United States
description_wiki: Bexar County ( BAIR or   BAY-ər; Spanish: Béxar [ˈbexaɾ]) is a county in the U.S. state of Texas.
The Texas State Teachers Association (TSTA) is a teacher's trade union based in Austin in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: American novelist and screenwriter (1934-1999)
description_wiki: William Weaks Morris (November 29, 1934 – August 2, 1999) was an American writer and editor born in Jackson, Mississippi and raised in Yazoo City, Mississippi.
